text.title: requirements-prioritization Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for requirements-prioritization as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Everything is marked Must. No boundaries. Team scope commitment is 150% of capacity.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: No real prioritization occurs; the team commits to 100% scope and blames engineering when the deadline is missed. Enforce the ~60% rule numerically: if Must > 60% of effort budget, reject and re-run with RICE.

failure-mode.description: Confidence inflation — teams default 100% on everything. No written justification per row.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: The Effort divisor stops doing work if Confidence is always high. Without justification, high-confidence scores masquerade as data-driven when they're guesses. Force the choice: 50% (no data), 80% (one data point), 100% (quantified baseline and delta). Make teams write it down.

failure-mode.description: HiPPO weighting — highest-paid voice anchors group sessions and reshapes consensus.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: The senior person scores 5, others follow. Elicit individually first, compute mean and σ. Reconcile only the high-σ rows. When the SVP walks in and says "that's a 5", the team's individual scores vanish. Use async scoring (Airtable form, Notion DB, CSV upload) to prevent this.
